description: Mobile app shell (v1.5.0). Give the HOME screen (index.html) a YouTube-mobile-app layout on PHONE-WIDTH viewports ONLY — desktop keeps today's header+sidebar unchanged (zero desktop regression; all new styling behind a mobile media query). Themed across all 4 eras x light/dark via the existing design tokens (--radius/--font-family/colors/etc.) — no hardcoded modern styling. TOP (mobile): centered FileTube logo (use /favicon.svg, the served icon) + a prominent search pill below it (reuse the existing #search-input behavior). BOTTOM: a fixed bottom nav bar, 4 items left->right: Home (the grid, /), Playlists (surfaces the mapped folders — the app already labels them 'Playlists (Folders)' — as tappable items, each linking to /?root=<folder>), Dark/Light (calls existing toggleTheme()), Settings (/setup.html). On mobile the current header-right (Settings link + moon toggle) folds into the bottom nav; the top simplifies to centered-logo + search. Bottom nav should appear on all mobile pages (index/setup/watch) for app-consistency (home is the priority; final scope in discovery). CRITICAL: (1) themed via tokens so it renders correctly in all 8 combos; (2) iOS PWA safe-area — the fixed bottom bar needs padding-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom) to clear the home indicator; (3) main content needs bottom padding so the fixed nav doesn't cover the last row; (4) active-state highlighting for current page/section; (5) zero desktop regression. Additive. Significant two-reviewer QA (theming across combos, desktop no-regression, iOS safe-area, active state).

history.note: Feature initiated via /kickoff. New feature mobile-app-shell (Mobile App Shell, v1.5.0). BOOTSTRAP ONLY: no discovery/design content produced and no specialist agents invoked this turn. Before bootstrapping, finalized the prior shipped feature: the Era Theme System (era-themes) shipped as v1.4.0. Copied docs/exec-plans/active/era-themes.md to docs/exec-plans/completed/era-themes.md with a COMPLETED/Shipped-v1.4.0 tombstone header, acceptance criteria checked off, and a 'Shipped v1.4.0' Progress-log entry. This toolset cannot true-`mv`, so the active copy at docs/exec-plans/active/era-themes.md remains as a stale tombstone to be `git rm`'d by the main loop. Checked for overlapping active exec plans: none block this — the only file in docs/exec-plans/active/ is the era-themes tombstone (being retired) plus .gitkeep. Checked the tech-debt tracker (docs/exec-plans/tech-debt-tracker.md): Active table empty; Closed #1 (unbounded transcode-cache, resolved by avi-ux-refinement) is unrelated to this UI/layout work. Tech-debt tracker left untouched. Overlap assessment with era-themes: mobile-app-shell explicitly BUILDS ON the v1.4.0 token system (must theme correctly across all 8 era x mode combos via existing tokens) — complementary, not conflicting; it adds a mobile shell layer, not a re-theming.

Bootstrapped and staged for discovery. Slug mobile-app-shell, v1.5.0 additive UI feature giving index.html a YouTube-mobile-app layout on PHONE-WIDTH viewports only, behind a mobile media query, with strict zero desktop regression (desktop header+sidebar unchanged). Description captured verbatim in state (top: centered FileTube logo via /favicon.svg + prominent search pill reusing #search-input behavior; bottom: fixed 4-item nav — Home / Playlists (mapped folders as /?root=<folder> tappable items, matching the app's existing 'Playlists (Folders)' label) / Dark-Light (existing toggleTheme()) / Settings (/setup.html); header-right Settings+moon fold into the bottom nav on mobile; bottom nav on all mobile pages index/setup/watch for app-consistency, home prioritized, final scope to be pinned in discovery). Flagged CRITICAL constraints: (1) themed via existing v1.4.0 design tokens so it renders correctly in all 8 era x mode combos, no hardcoded modern styling; (2) iOS PWA safe-area — fixed bottom bar needs padding-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom) to clear the home indicator; (3) main content needs bottom padding so the fixed nav doesn't cover the last grid row; (4) active-state highlighting for current page/section; (5) zero desktop regression. Significant two-reviewer QA required (theming across combos, desktop no-regression, iOS safe-area, active state). Artifacts remain null (fresh feature — no requirements/design/exec_plan yet). Awaiting /prep-pm-discover to route the product-manager. BOOTSTRAP ONLY this turn.

Discovery complete (product-manager). Exec plan written to docs/exec-plans/active/mobile-app-shell.md, grounded directly in public/index.html, public/setup.html, public/watch.html, public/js/main.js, public/js/common.js, and public/css/style.css (not just the bootstrap description). Key discovery decisions: (1) Playlists bottom-nav item opens a dedicated scoped folders panel/sheet reusing the existing folder data/links (/?root=<path>, folderSettings display names/hidden flag) rather than the full existing .sidebar, to avoid duplicating Home/Library-Settings links that already have their own bottom-nav entries; consequence — the mobile hamburger/#menu-toggle full-sidebar affordance must be reconciled (hidden on mobile) so there's exactly one path to folders. (2) Bottom nav ships on all three mobile pages (index/setup/watch) in v1.5.0 as one shared component; watch.html flagged as highest-care due to existing #resume-overlay/#transcode-overlay/#skip-controls z-index layering and its scrolling comments section. (3) Surfaced a concrete gap found while grounding in the code: none of the three pages set viewport-fit=cover on their viewport meta tag, so env(safe-area-inset-bottom) resolves to 0 on iOS regardless of CSS — brought into scope as a constraint/AC so the safe-area requirement is actually achievable. (4) No inline logo image exists today (header logo is a text logotype); mobile centered logo requires adding a small /favicon.svg image element, desktop unchanged. Acceptance criteria cover: bottom nav (4 items, mobile-only, token-themed), Playlists panel + hamburger reconciliation, iOS safe-area (env() + viewport-fit=cover + content bottom padding on all three pages), mobile top restructure (centered icon + search pill, header-right hidden), active-state per route (Home on / incl. ?search/?root, Settings on /setup.html, no false-positive on watch.html), and per-page rendering on index/setup/watch including watch's z-index/tap-interception constraints. Testability section is explicit that this is almost entirely manual/visual (no headless/visual-regression tooling in this repo) with a numbered manual QA checklist, and flags only the active-item resolver as a plausible pure/node:test-testable extraction if the principal-engineer designs it that way. Checked for conflicts: docs/exec-plans/active/ had only .gitkeep (no overlapping active plans); docs/exec-plans/tech-debt-tracker.md has no open items relevant to this UI work; no contradictions found with docs/ARCHITECTURE.md. Set artifacts.requirements and artifacts.exec_plan to docs/exec-plans/active/mobile-app-shell.md. Did not write code, tests, or design content — design section left for principal-engineer, task breakdown left for engineering-manager.

Two-reviewer pass. QA agent APPROVE-WITH-NITS (caught landscape nav/player overlap). Code-review workflow caught 2 show-stoppers QA missed: (1) Playlists sheet+backdrop shown on every mobile load (display:block overrode [hidden]) and undismissable; (2) missing safe-area-inset-top under viewport-fit=cover (header under notch). All fixed: sheet uses :not([hidden]); header padding-top/min-height + app-container padding-top get env(safe-area-inset-top); nav hidden in landscape (no player overlap, matches YouTube immersive); Playlists re-fetches each open (no stale latch); nav theme item synced via applyTheme. 86 tests green.
